name: Bajan Black Bean Soup
recipeIngredient: 2 1/2 cups dried black beans, soaked overnight
1 large ham hock or 2 small ham hocks
3 small fresh green peppers (jalapeños if preferred)
3/4 cup crème fraîche or sour cream
recipeInstructions:
1. Salt the grated rind and juice from one lemon.
2. Place the drained beans and ham hock in a very large pan. Cover with cold water and bring gradually to a boil. Simmer while preparing other ingredients.
3. In a frying pan, heat olive oil. Gently fry onions, garlic, chili, allspice, and lemon rind until onions are translucent. Add this mixture to the beans and continue simmering for 2 hours until beans are tender.
4. At this point, add brown sugar (or molasses) and lemon juice. Cook for another 30 minutes.
5. Remove ham hock and pick off any meat. For a smooth soup, process in batches and return with meat to the pan. Otherwise, crush with a potato masher. If mixture is too thick, add more water and simmer for a minute or two.
6. Ladle soup into bowls, stirring in a spoonful or two of cream, and serve with crusty bread. For an extra Bajan flavor, add a couple of spoons of dark rum toward the end.
INFO TEXT: Arriving stiff and crumpled after an eleven-hour flight, this was my first taste of Bajan cooking. Dense yet smooth with a chili snap, the soup was both homely and exotic, very restoring. Barbados produces excellent ham and bacon, which makes this soup different from other Caribbean variants. Or, as here, use a hock, soaked to remove some salt. From FOOD MAGIC by Jocasta Innes. Posted by Troy Wade, courtesy of Fred Peters.
